label: SES-116 / CNV-018 close-out — WS-012 orchestrator track delivered: PI-076 (area field), PI-077 (claim fields), PI-079 (ready-batches API), PI-082 (kickoff template), PI-078 (identifier reservation), PI-083 (area backfill tooling + deferred NOT NULL), PI-081 (orchestrator driver). Commits 290c37f, 61cc6e9, 743c935, 268e4a7, fbf08f3, 1866f72, f99d9ac on branch pi-076-area-field. Authors DEC-331. Resolves PI-076/077/078/079/082; addresses PI-081/083. Attaches to WS-012. Identifiers re-verify at apply time.

session.session_title: WS-012 orchestrator track — PI-076 through PI-083: area + claim fields on planning_items, ready-batches + identifier-reservation APIs, child kickoff template, area backfill tooling, and the orchestrator driver
session.session_description:
Single Claude Code execution at Doug's terminal. CNV-018. Parent workstream WS-012 (Parallel agent orchestrator and executive summary). Medium: chat (claude_code runner).

Seed: "I would like to work on pi-076 and related tasks." The session delivered the remaining open WS-012 orchestrator track — PI-076, 077, 078, 079, 081, 082, 083 (PI-080 was already Resolved; PI-074/075 exec-summary track already done).

Branch isolation: a parallel chat-UI orchestrator was actively committing and switching branches in the main working copy (it swept this session's first-saved files into a commit literally titled 'abandoned commit' and checked out pi-106-chat-slice-d-followups). To stay clear of it, all work was done in a dedicated git worktree (crmbuilder-pi076) on branch pi-076-area-field cut from origin/main (b13707c) — a clean pre-breakage baseline. The main working copy was left untouched.

Delivered (seven commits, see commits[]):
1. PI-076 (290c37f): nullable JSON `area` column on planning_items (migration 0024) + structural CHECK + an 18-value `area` vocabulary + access-layer validator + Pydantic + tests.
2. PI-077 (61cc6e9): claimed_by/claimed_at (migration 0025, both-or-neither CHECK) + claim/release access helpers (optimistic concurrency) + claim/release endpoints.
3. PI-079 (743c935): GET /orchestration/ready-batches — blocked_by topological depth batching with area/claimed_by attached, cycle bucket, area + max_depth filters.
4. PI-082 (268e4a7): child-agent kickoff template with a documented placeholder contract.
5. PI-078 (fbf08f3): POST /identifiers/reserve (migration 0026 + reservations table) — atomic identifier blocks with TTL auto-release and implicit consume.
6. PI-083 (1866f72): area backfill tooling (propose/apply) + the NOT NULL tightening migration parked under migrations/deferred/ (out of the alembic chain until backfill runs).
7. PI-081 (f99d9ac): the orchestrator driver — pure area-disjoint wave clustering + kickoff rendering + file lock + a fully-functional --dry-run; live --execute is guarded as the human-driven WS-012 acceptance test.

Validation: migrations 0024-0026 chain cleanly onto a copy of the live CRMBUILDER.db (106 rows preserved, CHECKs enforced); all new access/api/migration/scripts tests green (~90 added). The ~120 pre-existing non-UI baseline failures are the PI-073 session-schema debt in test fixtures, unrelated to this work.

Resolves PI-076/077/078/079/082. Addresses PI-081 (its live --execute run is the acceptance test) and PI-083 (its live area backfill + NOT NULL activation are Doug-gated follow-ups). Authored DEC-331. In flight at end: commits sit on pi-076-area-field awaiting Doug's review/push; this close-out's identifiers (SES-116/CNV-018/DEC-331) must be re-verified against live heads at apply time (parallel sessions are active) and re-keyed if claimed.
session.session_executive_summary: Delivered the remaining WS-012 parallel-agent orchestrator track in one Claude Code session, working in an isolated git worktree off origin/main to avoid a concurrently running orchestrator. Seven commits: PI-076 (area field on planning_items), PI-077 (claim fields + claim/release), PI-079 (ready-batches API), PI-082 (child kickoff template), PI-078 (identifier reservation API), PI-083 (area backfill tooling + a deferred NOT NULL migration), and PI-081 (the orchestrator driver — dry-run complete, live execute guarded as the human acceptance test). Migrations 0024-0026 validated against a copy of the live CRMBUILDER DB; ~90 new tests pass. Resolves PI-076/077/078/079/082; addresses PI-081 and PI-083, whose live-run and live-backfill steps are Doug-gated follow-ups.

conversation.conversation_title: Building the WS-012 orchestrator foundation end to end (PI-076–083)
conversation.conversation_purpose: Deliver the remaining open WS-012 orchestrator track: the planning_item schema extensions (area, claim), the orchestration + reservation APIs, the child kickoff template, the area backfill tooling, and the orchestrator driver.
conversation.conversation_description: Single-conversation Claude Code session that built the open WS-012 orchestrator track (PI-076–083) in an isolated worktree off origin/main. Added the area field and claim fields to planning_items, the ready-batches and identifier-reservation APIs, the child-agent kickoff template, the area-backfill tooling (with a deferred NOT NULL migration), and the orchestrator driver (dry-run complete; live execute guarded). Validated against a copy of the live DB; ~90 new tests green. Resolves PI-076/077/078/079/082; addresses PI-081/083.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-076 — add area field to planning_items (multi-valued JSON, vocabulary-checked)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-076 — add area field to planning_items (multi-valued JSON, vocabulary-checked)

Adds a nullable JSON `area` column to planning_items so each item can
declare which codebase/methodology areas it touches, enabling the
parallel-agent orchestrator (WS-012) to partition the open backlog into
file-disjoint clusters (DEC-246 area-level parallelism; DEC-247
multi-valued area sets).

- migration 0024: nullable JSON column + structural CHECK (valid
  non-empty array). Element-level vocabulary is enforced at the access
  layer because SQLite CHECK constraints cannot iterate array elements.
- vocab.AREAS: the 18 area labels from orchestrator-planning.md §2.1
  (the prose says "seventeen" but enumerates 18; the names are
  authoritative, so all 18 are registered).
- _helpers.validate_optional_value_list + access-layer enforcement on
  planning_item create/update (non-empty, distinct, string, in-vocab).
- Pydantic `area` on create/update; the snapshot exporter is generic.
- JSON(none_as_null=True) so Python None stores as SQL NULL (otherwise
  the text 'null' fails the `area IS NULL` arm of the CHECK).

NOT NULL is deferred to PI-083 (area backfill on currently-open items).
Validated: 61 access/api tests green; migration 0024 applies cleanly to
a copy of the live CRMBUILDER.db (0023 -> 0024, 106 rows preserved).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-077 — add claimed_by/claimed_at to planning_items + claim/release
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-077 — add claimed_by/claimed_at to planning_items + claim/release

Lets the orchestrator (WS-012) mark a planning item as held by a
specific agent so two agents never grab the same item. Resolves the
design-doc open question (orchestrator-planning.md §6) in favour of the
conversation identifier (CONV-NNN) as the claim-holder per DEC-248 (the
conversation is the unit of coherent work).

- migration 0025: nullable claimed_by (TEXT) + claimed_at (DATETIME) with
  a both-or-neither pairing CHECK. No backfill — existing rows stay null.
- model columns + ck_planning_claim_pairing.
- access helpers claim_planning_item / release_planning_item with
  optimistic concurrency: claim fails (409) if held by a different
  claimant, is idempotent for the same claimant; release optionally
  guarded by claimant. Race-safe under the engine's BEGIN IMMEDIATE.
- POST /planning-items/{id}/claim and /release endpoints + request models.

Validated: 46 access/api tests green; migrations 0024+0025 chain cleanly
onto a copy of the live CRMBUILDER.db, pairing CHECK enforced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2: PI-079 — GET /orchestration/ready-batches endpoint

The single call the orchestrator makes at the start of every run to learn
what is safe to dispatch. Returns open planning items grouped by
dependency depth, each carrying identifier/title/executive_summary/area/
claimed_by, plus a cyclic bucket and warnings.

- access/orchestration.compute_ready_batches: builds the blocked_by graph
  (planning_item -> planning_item) counting only open blockers (a
  blocked_by edge to a resolved item is treated as satisfied), computes
  longest-path depth via DFS, surfaces cycles (and anything transitively
  depending on one) in a separate cyclic bucket rather than failing.
  Optional area filter (intersection; vocab-validated) and max_depth cutoff.
- GET /orchestration/ready-batches router, registered in main.

Depends on PI-076 (area) + PI-077 (claimed_by). 14 access/api tests green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-082 — child-agent kickoff prompt template
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-082 — child-agent kickoff prompt template

The standardized kickoff each child agent receives when the orchestrator
dispatches it. Markdown template at
PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/orchestrator/child-agent-kickoff-template.md
with a documented {{placeholder}} contract the driver (PI-081) fills at
dispatch time.

Body sections per PI-082: operating mode (DETAIL default), engagement +
workstream context, pre-allocated identifiers, planning items in scope
(full inlined descriptions from ready-batches), areas claimed + the hard
conflict rule (stay inside claimed areas), close-out payload requirements
(own nine-section ses_NNN.json + apply prompt), branch/commit
conventions. Notes that the orchestrator — not the child — writes the
conversation_orchestrates_conversation edge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2: PI-078 — POST /identifiers/reserve endpoint

Lets the orchestrator atomically reserve a block of prefixed identifiers
(e.g. the next five SES) at the start of a run so concurrent child agents
never race on next-available numbers when posting close-out records.

- migration 0026 + IdentifierReservation model: one row per reserved
  block (entity_type, reserved_identifiers, max_number, reserved_by,
  reserved_at, expires_at). Ephemeral runtime state — deliberately not in
  the snapshot exporter.
- access/repositories/identifier_reservations.reserve: head =
  max(table_max, active_reservation_max) + 1, reusing each entity type's
  canonical next-identifier function and parsing prefix/width from it
  (so CM-0001 width-4 etc. are respected). Once a reserved id becomes a
  row, table_max covers it (implicit consume); expired blocks are purged
  and ignored (TTL auto-release, default 1h). Reservable: session,
  decision, planning_item, risk, topic, conversation (charter/status are
  version singletons, references are numeric — excluded).
- POST /identifiers/reserve router + IdentifierReserveIn schema.

15 access/api tests green; migrations 0024-0026 chain cleanly on a copy
of the live CRMBUILDER.db.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-083 — area backfill tooling + deferred NOT NULL migration
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-083 — area backfill tooling + deferred NOT NULL migration

PI-083 requires a Doug spot-check and mutates live governance data, so
this lands the tooling and gates the live steps:

- scripts/backfill_pi_083_area.py: two-phase, Doug-in-the-loop.
  `propose` fetches every Open planning item with a null area, infers
  area set(s) via a deterministic keyword/path heuristic (infer_areas,
  never proposes an unregistered area), and writes an editable proposal
  JSON. `apply` PATCHes areas from the reviewed proposal. Items with no
  heuristic signal are flagged needs_review.
- migrations/deferred/0027_*_area_not_null.py: the NOT NULL tightening,
  parked OUT of the active chain (alembic does not scan deferred/) since
  every row starts null; it guards against NULL rows and documents the
  git-mv activation procedure. README explains the deferred convention.

Not resolving PI-083: the live backfill PATCH + NOT NULL activation are
Doug-gated follow-ups. 7 heuristic tests green; alembic head unchanged
at 0026 (deferred migration confirmed out of chain).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-081 — orchestrator driver script (dry-run complete; execute guarded)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-081 — orchestrator driver script (dry-run complete; execute guarded)

The capstone driver under crmbuilder-v2/scripts/orchestrator/. Reads the
ready-batches API (PI-079), plans area-disjoint child clusters, reserves
identifiers (PI-078), claims items (PI-077), and renders a kickoff per
child from the PI-082 template.

- planning.py: pure wave partitioning. Connected components over "shares
  an area" (union-find) => one cluster (one child agent) per component;
  claimed items skipped, area-less items set aside as unparallelisable.
  assert_clusters_disjoint guards parallel safety before dispatch.
- kickoff.py: pure template rendering — substitute {{markers}}, strip the
  contract comment, inline planning-item descriptions; fails loud on
  missing/leftover placeholders.
- run.py: CLI. --dry-run (DEFAULT, safe) does pre-flight + fetch + plan +
  renders every child kickoff to --out-dir with no reserve/claim/spawn.
  File-lock (~/.crmbuilder-v2/) ensures a single orchestrator. --execute
  is the human-driven WS-012 acceptance test (design doc §7) and refuses
  to run silently until the local Claude Code spawn invocation is wired.

16 pure-core tests green (clustering, rendering, plan/summary, real
template contract). A live dry-run against the API and the first real
--execute run are Doug-driven follow-ups (require the API restarted with
this code; --execute spawns real subagents).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: Parallel-agent orchestrator foundation — claimed_by references the conversation identifier; 18-value area vocabulary; live NOT NULL and --execute deferred
decisions.context: The WS-012 orchestrator track (PI-076–083) left one design question open in orchestrator-planning.md §6 (whether claimed_by references a conversation or a session identifier) and required two scope calls about what to finish now vs defer to Doug-gated follow-ups.
decisions.decision: (1) planning_items.claimed_by holds the conversation identifier (CNV-NNN). (2) The `area` vocabulary registers all 18 enumerated names from orchestrator-planning.md §2.1 (the prose says 'seventeen' but lists eighteen; names win). (3) The area column's NOT NULL tightening is parked as a deferred migration (migrations/deferred/0027_*) out of the alembic chain until PI-083's backfill gives every open item an area; the orchestrator's live --execute path is implemented but guarded — the first real run is the human-driven acceptance test per the design doc §7.
decisions.rationale: Per DEC-248 each child agent owns one conversation + one session, and the conversation is the unit of coherent work, so it is the semantically accurate claim-holder. The 18 enumerated area names are the authoritative source over a miscounted prose word. Deferring NOT NULL avoids a chain-breaking migration on a DB whose rows all start null; deferring --execute avoids spawning a real multi-agent swarm before a human can supervise the first run.
decisions.alternatives_considered: claimed_by = session identifier (simpler to write at start-of-work) — rejected as less semantically accurate. Dropping one area to honor the 'seventeen' count — rejected as arbitrary. Adding NOT NULL now — rejected: it would fail on the all-null existing rows and block the migration chain. Auto-running --execute — rejected: unsupervised swarm spawn is unsafe for a first run.
decisions.consequences: claimed_by stores CNV-NNN as a free string (no cross-table FK). Adding a future area requires editing vocab.AREAS only. Activating NOT NULL is a documented git-mv of the deferred migration after backfill. The driver's --execute spawn invocation must be wired to the local Claude Code setup before the first real run.
decisions.executive_summary: Records the foundational design calls for the WS-012 parallel-agent orchestrator, built across PI-076–083. (1) Resolves the orchestrator-planning.md §6 open question: a planning item's claimed_by holds the CONVERSATION identifier (CNV-NNN), not the session identifier — per DEC-248 the conversation is the unit of coherent work that holds the claim. (2) The `area` vocabulary has 18 values: orchestrator-planning.md §2.1 and the PI-076 text both say 'seventeen' but enumerate eighteen names, and the enumerated names are authoritative. (3) Two completion steps are deferred and Doug-gated: the area column's NOT NULL tightening (parked as a deferred migration until PI-083's backfill runs) and the orchestrator's live --execute run (guarded as the human-driven acceptance test per the design doc §7).
